```json
[
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Network Expansion Acceleration",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap public API for active charger counts by region, daily snapshots",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Rapid week-over-week growth in operational EV charging infrastructure signals infrastructure investment cycle acceleration and policy tailwinds, driving energy and industrial demand.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "EV charger buildout requires industrial equipment, electrical contractors, and grid upgrades; correlates with industrials capex cycles."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly charger count growth exceeds 1.2% and 20-day moving average of daily growth rate is positive, enter long",
      "exit": "Exit after 10 trading days or if weekly growth drops below 0.3% for 2 consecutive weeks",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Charger networks expand continuously; weekly growth crossing 1.2% threshold occurs 2–4 times per quarter in major markets."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Earthquake Cluster Economic Disruption Trade",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "USGS earthquake activity API, magnitude 4.5+ events within major US metros, daily updates",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Clusters of moderate seismic activity in economically active regions trigger insurance and property damage expectations, boosting financials and industrials short-term positioning.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "Earthquake clusters drive insurance payouts, reinsurance demand, and property damage claims that benefit financial services and adjust valuations."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 3+ magnitude 4.5+ earthquakes occur within 500km radius in 7 days, enter long financial sector",
      "exit": "Exit after 12 trading days or once seismic activity returns to baseline (no events in 5 days)",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "US experiences 1–2 magnitude 4.5+ earthquake clusters per month; regional clustering within 7 days happens monthly on average."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Freight Diesel Volatility Spike Contraction Signal",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series GASDESW (diesel prices) and port_container_volume adapter (weekly TEU data), daily/weekly updates",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When diesel prices spike >5% week-over-week while container volumes contract >3% weekly, supply chain tightening creates logistics margin pressure and industrial demand signals.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Diesel spikes + volume contractions signal demand shock and rising cost pressures on transport and manufacturing."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If GASDESW spike >5% WoW and port TEU volumes fall >3% WoW simultaneously, enter short industrials",
      "exit": "Exit after 8 trading days or if either metric normalizes (diesel falls back <2% or TEU growth turns positive)",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Diesel and port volume volatility cross thresholds 2–3 times per quarter; simultaneous moves occur monthly or near-monthly."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Google Trends Job Search Panic Spike",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search interest for 'how to write a resume' and 'interview tips,' normalized to baseline",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sharp spikes in job-search-related Google searches signal layoff fears or employment anxiety, causing consumer spending pullbacks and volatility in discretionary sectors.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Job search anxiety correlates with consumer confidence collapse and discretionary spending cuts."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If either 'resume' or 'interview tips' search volume jumps >40% week-over-week above 12-week moving average, short discretionary",
      "exit": "Exit after 10 trading days or if search interest returns to <110% of 12-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Job-search Google Trends spike above 40% threshold 3–5 times per quarter due to weekly earnings seasons, layoff news cycles, and seasonal hiring patterns."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Weather Extreme Utility Demand Whipsaw",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Open-Meteo daily temperature extremes (max/min) for top 20 US metros, aggregated deviation from 30-year normal",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Extreme temperature swings (>15°F deviation from seasonal normal across multiple regions) drive sudden utility demand spikes and grid stress, boosting energy and utilities sector volatility.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLU",
      "rationale": "Weather extremes force emergency grid operations and spot market pricing spikes, benefiting utilities operators."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 8+ metros show temperature deviation >15°F from 30-year normal on same day, enter long utilities",
      "exit": "Exit after 6 trading days or if temperature volatility normalizes (deviations <8°F for 3 consecutive days)",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "daily",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Synchronized multi-region temperature extremes occur 8–12 times per year; seasonal transitions (spring/fall) generate daily crossings."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Healthcare Cost Search Anxiety Surge",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search interest for 'prescription drug prices' and 'medical bill debt,' normalized to 12-week baseline",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in healthcare cost searches signal consumer stress about medical expenses, often preceding healthcare sector volatility and consumer spending pullbacks.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLV",
      "rationale": "Healthcare cost anxiety may depress elective procedures and consumer pharmaceutical adherence, pressuring healthcare revenues."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If either search term jumps >50% WoW above 12-week MA, short healthcare",
      "exit": "Exit after 9 trading days or if search interest normalizes to <115% of 12-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Healthcare cost search spikes >50% occur 2–4 times per quarter, especially around earnings seasons, policy announcements, and seasonal insurance renewal cycles."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Attention Spike: Supply Chain Bottleneck News Volume",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "RSS feed article count monitoring 'supply chain disruption' and 'logistics backlog' across major financial news sites, daily updates",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Daily spikes in supply chain disruption news coverage correlate with industrial demand shocks and equity rotation into defensive sectors.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Supply chain news spikes signal production headwinds and cost pressures that depress industrial equipment and manufacturing valuations."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily RSS article count for supply chain keywords exceeds 150% of 20-day moving average, short industrials",
      "exit": "Exit after 7 trading days or if RSS count returns to <120% of 20-day MA for 2 consecutive days",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Supply chain news spikes above 150% threshold occur 3–6 times per quarter due to port congestion, carrier bankruptcies, and seasonal demand shocks."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Small Cap Volatility Compression Breakout",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "Yahoo Finance daily price data for IWM (Russell 2000) and SPY, 30-day realized volatility, price_only adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When IWM 30-day realized volatility falls below 12% while IWM lags SPY by >2% cumulatively over 15 days, subsequent mean reversion often triggers sharp small-cap rallies.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Small-cap outperformance correlates with risk-on rotation and consumer discretionary strength."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If IWM 30-day realized vol <12% AND IWM underperforms SPY by >2% over prior 15 days AND IWM closes above 5-day MA, enter long",
      "exit": "Exit after 8 trading days or once IWM outperforms SPY by >1.5% over 3 days",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Small-cap volatility compression + underperformance occurs 3–4 times per quarter, especially after Fed pauses or risk sentiment shifts."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Commercial Real Estate Financing Stress Google Trends",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search interest for 'commercial mortgage rates' and 'CMBS default,' normalized to 12-week baseline",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in commercial real estate financing search terms signal financial distress in office/retail property markets, pressuring REIT and financial valuations.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLRE",
      "rationale": "CRE financing stress directly impacts REIT returns and commercial property values."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If either search term spikes >45% WoW above 12-week MA, short real estate",
      "exit": "Exit after 10 trading days or if search interest normalizes to <120% of 12-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Commercial mortgage and CMBS stress searches spike >45% WoW 2–3 times per quarter, driven by rate cycle turns and quarterly earnings disappointments."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Energy Sector Relative Strength Momentum Fade",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLE and SPY, relative strength index (XLE vs SPY 20-day momentum), price_only adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When XLE outperforms SPY by >4% over 10 days but XLE daily close falls below its 5-day moving average, mean reversion pullback often follows within days.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Energy momentum exhaustion signals profit-taking and demand concerns in commodity-linked sectors."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If XLE leads SPY >4% over 10 days AND XLE closes below 5-day MA AND MACD(12,26) turns negative, short energy",
      "exit": "Exit after 6 trading days or if XLE closes above 10-day MA",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Energy outperformance spikes >4% over 10-day windows occur 3–5 times per quarter; momentum exhaustion patterns fire weekly."
  }
]
```
